trans-unit.source:
RESTRICTED DOCUMENT: TOWER-001 – Director-Level Only
TOWER is not a crisis. A crisis implies the possibility of resolution. TOWER is a condition in which the distinction between Shards ceases to be [DEGRADED] and all possible realities attempt to occupy the same [DEGRADED] simultaneously.
Instance A (Ash Shard, pre-collapse): The sky began displaying weather from adjacent Shards. Rain fell upward. Snow was warm. The Ash Shard collapsed within fourteen days. Bureau assessment: "the Shard forgot which set of physics it was using."
Instance B (Threshold Palace, Voss expedition): Architecture "trying to be all buildings at once." Walls simultaneously stone, coral, concrete, wood, and a material described as "solid argument." Voss [DEGRADED] on Day 4,017.
Instance C (In progress): [ENTIRE SECTION DEGRADED. The phrase "it is already too late to prevent" appears seventeen times. The phrase "but not too late to understand" appears once.]
RECOMMENDED ACTION: [CONSUMED]
DIRECTOR'S NOTE: The recommended action section has been consumed by the document itself. The previous version read: "Observe. Record. Do not look away. The multiverse survives because someone is [CONSUMED]." We are choosing to interpret this as encouragement.
trans-unit.target:
RESTRICTED DOCUMENT: TOWER-001 – Director-Level Only
TOWER is not a crisis. A crisis implies the possibility of resolution. TOWER is a condition in which the distinction between Shards ceases to be [DEGRADED] and all possible realities attempt to occupy the same [DEGRADED] simultaneously.

Instance C (In progress): [ENTIRE SECTION DEGRADED. The phrase "it is already too late to prevent" appears seventeen times. The phrase "but not too late to understand" appears once.]
RECOMMENDED ACTION: [CONSUMED]
DIRECTOR'S NOTE: The recommended action section has been consumed by the document itself. The previous version read: "Observe. Record. Do not look away. The multiverse survives because someone is [CONSUMED]." We are choosing to interpret this as encouragement.

trans-unit.source:
This section has been [CONSUMED] seventeen times. Each time, a Cartographer rewrites it. Each time, the document eats it. We persist.
What the Bureau will not tell you is this: the Shards are not random. They are not the arbitrary debris of a cosmic accident. They are an argument – positions in a philosophical dispute that the Unnamed could not resolve while it was whole. Control (Velgarien). Wonder (the Gaslit Reach). Knowledge (Station Null). Hope (Speranza). Voice (the Cite des Dames). These were the first positions. They were not the last.
The Fracture was not a catastrophe. It was a vote. The universe asked itself what mattered, and it could not agree, and the disagreement became geography. The vote is not over. New questions arise – questions the existing Shards cannot answer – and where the question is sharp enough, where it cuts deeply enough into the fabric of the possible, new geography crystallizes. A new Shard. A new world. A new position in the argument.
The Bureau does not know how many Shards there are. The Bureau does not believe the question has a fixed answer. The Fracture is not a past-tense event with a countable number of consequences. It is an ongoing condition – the universe still disagreeing with itself, still producing new realities at the edges of the map.
If this is true – and the Bureau does not confirm it, because confirmation would imply understanding, and understanding would imply the possibility of resolution, and resolution is the one thing the Bureau exists to [CONSUMED] – then the Convergence is not the end of the argument. It is the next ballot. The known Shards pressing together. All the answers colliding – the old ones and the ones that have not yet been asked. And somewhere in the collision, the possibility of an answer that contains all the others.
The Bureau has a name for this answer. The name is classified. The classification has been [CONSUMED]. The [CONSUMED] has been filed under "Things That Are Also Other Things." The file is growing.
[ADDENDUM, HANDWRITTEN IN THE MARGIN IN INK THAT IS STILL WET]: "The file is not growing. The file is becoming. There is a difference. Growing implies addition. Becoming implies change. The Bureau is changing. The Bureau has always been changing. The Bureau is the next answer, and the next answer is not an answer at all. It is the question, asked properly, for the first time."
[THE ADDENDUM HAS BEEN CLASSIFIED. THE CLASSIFICATION HAS BEEN CONSUMED. THE INK IS STILL WET.]
trans-unit.target:

trans-unit.source:
Every Shard is an answer. Velgarien answers: "What if control were absolute?" The Gaslit Reach answers: "What if the darkness were kind?" Station Null answers: "What if we could see the wound?" Speranza answers: "What if we simply refused to stop?" The Cite des Dames answers: "What if women had always been heard?" And somewhere at the edges of the map, new Shards are crystallizing around questions that have not yet been named.
Each answer is wrong. Each answer is necessary. The multiverse exists because no single answer is sufficient, and the Fracture was the universe's way of admitting this. The Fracture continues to be the universe's way of admitting this. It has not stopped admitting it.
The Convergence – the event that the Bureau monitors, that the ruins foretell, that TOWER threatens – is not the end. It is the next question. When enough Shards have touched, when enough Bleeds have flowed, when enough agents have dreamed of worlds they've never visited, the multiverse will reach a decision point: merge, or differentiate further. Collapse into one answer, or fracture into a thousand more.
The Cartographers do not know which outcome is correct. They know only this: someone must be watching when it happens. Someone must be standing at the edge with a map and a compass and the willingness to draw a new line on a chart that has no edges.
That someone, in the cosmology of the Cartographers, in the deep mythology of the Bureau of Impossible Geography, in the restricted files and the consumed pages and the maps that are also doors – that someone is whoever is reading this.
Shattered worlds. Bleeding borders. The map has no edges. Which world do you enter first?
trans-unit.target:
Every Shard is an answer. Velgarien answers: "What if control were absolute?" The Gaslit Reach answers: "What if the darkness were kind?" Station Null answers: "What if we could see the wound?" And somewhere at the edges of the map, new Shards are crystallizing around questions that have not yet been named.
Each answer is wrong. Each answer is necessary. The multiverse exists because no single answer is sufficient, and the Fracture was the universe's way of admitting this. The Fracture continues to be the universe's way of admitting this. It has not stopped admitting it.
The Convergence – the event that the Bureau monitors, that the ruins foretell, that TOWER threatens – is not the end. It is the next question. That someone, in the cosmology of the Cartographers, in the deep mythology of the Bureau of Impossible Geography, in the restricted files and the consumed pages and the maps that are also doors – that someone is whoever is reading this.
Shattered worlds. Bleeding borders. The map has no edges. Which world do you enter first?

trans-unit.source: Memory is not storage. Memory is existence.
trans-unit.target: Erinnerung ist keine Speicherung. Erinnerung ist Existenz.

trans-unit.source: residual substrate displacement – monitoring decay curve
multiple tremors entering decay phase – substrate settling
trans-unit.target: Mehrere Beben in Abklingphase – Substrate beruhigt sich

trans-unit.source: severe substrate distortion – local geometry unreliable
concurrent substrate fractures – recommend caution in affected zones
trans-unit.target: Gleichzeitige Substrate-Frakturen – Vorsicht in betroffenen Zonen empfohlen

trans-unit.source: overlapping tremor signatures – interference patterns forming
active substrate displacement – affected zones may behave unpredictably
trans-unit.target: Aktive Substrate-Verschiebung – betroffene Zonen können unberechenbar reagieren

trans-unit.source: multiple signatures detected – the substrate is restless
trans-unit.target: Mehrfachsignaturen erkannt – das Substrate ist unruhig

trans-unit.source: How many times an echo can re-echo (1-3). Depth 1: direct echo only. Depth 2: echoes of echoes. Depth 3: maximum – reality fractures deeply. Each depth level applies strength decay.
trans-unit.target: Wie oft ein Echo sich wiederholen kann (1–3). Tiefe 1: nur direktes Echo. Tiefe 2: Echos von Echos. Tiefe 3: Maximum – Realität bricht tiefgreifend. Jede Tiefenstufe bewirkt Stärkeabnahme.

trans-unit.source: Clearance granted – welcome to the Forge, Architect! tokens await you.
trans-unit.target: Freigabe erteilt – willkommen in der Forge, Architekt! Tokens erwarten dich.

trans-unit.source: Clearance granted – welcome to the Forge, Architect
trans-unit.target: Freigabe erteilt – willkommen in der Forge, Architekt

trans-unit.source: No AI usage data recorded yet. Usage tracking starts when ai_usage_log receives its first entry.
trans-unit.target: Noch keine KI-Nutzungsdaten erfasst. Die Erfassung beginnt mit dem ersten Eintrag in ai_usage_log.

trans-unit.source: Low influence. Assign to a building matching their profession to increase readiness.
trans-unit.target: Geringer Einfluss. Weise den Agenten einem Gebäude zu, das zu seinem Beruf passt, um die Einsatzbereitschaft zu erhöhen.

trans-unit.source: Overall emotional state from −100 (distressed) to +100 (euphoric). Computed from all active moodlets. Affects operative success in epochs.
trans-unit.target: Allgemeiner emotionaler Zustand von −100 (verzweifelt) bis +100 (euphorisch). Wird aus allen aktiven Moodlets berechnet. Beeinflusst den Operativerfolgswert in Epochen.

trans-unit.source: Stress accumulates when mood is negative and recovers when positive. Above 800: agent breakdown – a crisis event that spreads anxiety to nearby agents. Affects operative success (−3% above 500).
trans-unit.target: Stress akkumuliert bei negativer Stimmung und erholt sich bei positiver. Über 800: Zusammenbruch des Agenten – ein Krisenereignis, das Angst auf nahegelegene Agenten überträgt. Beeinflusst den Operativerfolgswert (−3 % über 500).

trans-unit.source: Five core drives (0–100) that decay over time and are fulfilled by activities. Low needs push agents toward specific behaviors. Social: interaction craving. Purpose: meaningful work. Safety: zone security. Comfort: building quality. Stimulation: novelty.
trans-unit.target: Fünf Kernantriebe (0–100), die mit der Zeit abnehmen und durch Aktivitäten erfüllt werden. Niedrige Bedürfnisse treiben Agenten zu bestimmten Verhaltensweisen. Sozial: Interaktionsbedürfnis. Bestimmung: sinnvolle Arbeit. Sicherheit: Zonensicherheit. Komfort: Gebäudequalität. Stimulation: Neuartigkeit.

trans-unit.source: Individual mood modifiers from events, social interactions, and zone conditions. Each has a strength (±20) and a decay type: permanent (gray dot), timed (yellow dot, expires at set time), or decaying (blue dot, strength fades gradually).
trans-unit.target: Individuelle Stimmungsmodifikatoren durch Ereignisse, soziale Interaktionen und Zonenbedingungen. Jeder hat eine Stärke (±20) und einen Verfallstyp: permanent (grauer Punkt), zeitlich begrenzt (gelber Punkt, läuft ab), oder abklingend (blauer Punkt, Stärke nimmt ab).

trans-unit.source: Agent autonomy may be disabled for this simulation
trans-unit.target: Agentenautonomie ist möglicherweise für diese Simulation deaktiviert

trans-unit.source: When Agent Autonomy is active, your simulation lives between visits. Agents develop moods, form opinions, pursue activities, argue, celebrate, and break down under stress. The Utility AI selects actions based on personality, needs, and social context. Autonomous events emerge from threshold conditions and are narrated by AI. The world accumulates history through every tick.
trans-unit.target: Wenn die Agentenautonomie aktiv ist, lebt deine Simulation zwischen Besuchen weiter. Agenten entwickeln Stimmungen, bilden Meinungen, verfolgen Aktivitäten, streiten, feiern und brechen unter Stress zusammen. Die Utility-KI wählt Aktionen basierend auf Persönlichkeit, Bedürfnissen und sozialem Kontext. Autonome Ereignisse entstehen aus Schwellenwertbedingungen und werden von KI erzählt. Die Welt akkumuliert Geschichte durch jeden Tick.

trans-unit.source: Real-world weather data from your simulation's geographic anchor influences zone atmosphere and agent mood every heartbeat tick. Prague fog drifts through Velgarien's government quarter, Arctic storms batter Station Null, Mediterranean heat parches Speranza. Over 1,000 bilingual template fragments compose unique narrative descriptions that never repeat. Zero AI cost.
trans-unit.target: Echtzeit-Wetterdaten vom geografischen Anker deiner Simulation beeinflussen die Zonenatmosphäre und Agentenstimmung bei jedem Heartbeat-Tick. Prager Nebel zieht durch Velgariens Regierungsviertel, arktische Stürme peitschen Station Null, Mittelmeerhitze dörrt Speranza aus. Über 1.000 zweisprachige Vorlagenfragmente komponieren einzigartige Erzählungen, die sich nie wiederholen. Null KI-Kosten.

trans-unit.source: The Living World system makes agents act autonomously between your visits. Agents develop moods, form opinions about each other, pursue activities based on their personality, and generate social events. When you return, the world has lived – relationships shifted, conflicts emerged, celebrations happened. A morning briefing summarizes what occurred.
trans-unit.target: Das System der Lebendigen Welt lässt Agenten zwischen deinen Besuchen autonom handeln. Agenten entwickeln Stimmungen, bilden Meinungen übereinander, verfolgen Aktivitäten basierend auf ihrer Persönlichkeit und erzeugen soziale Ereignisse. Wenn du zurückkehrst, hat die Welt gelebt – Beziehungen haben sich verschoben, Konflikte sind entstanden, Feste wurden gefeiert. Ein Morgenbriefing fasst zusammen, was geschehen ist.

trans-unit.source: The system runs during each heartbeat tick (every 4 hours). It processes agent needs, updates moods, recalculates opinions, selects activities via Utility AI, generates social interactions between co-located agents, and triggers autonomous events when thresholds are crossed. Rule-based mechanics cost zero AI calls; only narrative generation uses LLM.
trans-unit.target: Das System läuft bei jedem Heartbeat-Tick (alle 4 Stunden). Es verarbeitet Agentenbedürfnisse, aktualisiert Stimmungen, berechnet Meinungen neu, wählt Aktivitäten per Utility-KI, generiert soziale Interaktionen zwischen Agenten am gleichen Ort und löst autonome Ereignisse aus, wenn Schwellenwerte überschritten werden. Regelbasierte Mechaniken kosten null KI-Aufrufe; nur Erzählgenerierung nutzt LLM.

trans-unit.source: Every agent has a mood score (−100 to +100) computed from active moodlets – individual emotional influences with three decay types. Permanent moodlets persist forever. Timed moodlets expire after a set duration. Decaying moodlets gradually weaken over time. The dominant emotion (joy, anxiety, anger, grief) is determined by the strongest active moodlet.
trans-unit.target: Jeder Agent hat einen Stimmungswert (−100 bis +100), berechnet aus aktiven Moodlets – individuellen emotionalen Einflüssen mit drei Verfallstypen. Permanente Moodlets bestehen für immer. Zeitlich begrenzte Moodlets laufen nach einer festgelegten Dauer ab. Abklingende Moodlets verlieren allmählich an Stärke. Die vorherrschende Emotion (Freude, Angst, Wut, Trauer) wird durch das stärkste aktive Moodlet bestimmt.

trans-unit.source: Stress accumulates when mood is negative and recovers when positive. Resilient agents (low neuroticism) recover faster. When stress exceeds 800, the agent experiences a breakdown – a crisis event that spreads anxiety moodlets to all agents in the same zone, potentially triggering cascade breakdowns.
trans-unit.target: Stress akkumuliert bei negativer Stimmung und erholt sich bei positiver. Resiliente Agenten (niedrige Neurotizismus) erholen sich schneller. Wenn Stress 800 überschreitet, erlebt der Agent einen Zusammenbruch – ein Krisenereignis, das Angst-Moodlets an alle Agenten in derselben Zone verbreitet und potenziell Kettenreaktionen auslöst.

trans-unit.source: Agents have five core needs that decay over time: Social (craving interaction), Purpose (meaningful work), Safety (zone security), Comfort (building conditions), and Stimulation (novelty). Each need ranges from 0 (desperate) to 100 (fully satisfied). Decay rates are personalized based on personality – extraverts lose social faster, conscientious agents need purpose more urgently.
trans-unit.target: Agenten haben fünf Kernbedürfnisse, die mit der Zeit abnehmen: Sozial (Interaktionsbedürfnis), Bestimmung (sinnvolle Arbeit), Sicherheit (Zonensicherheit), Komfort (Gebäudezustand) und Stimulation (Neuartigkeit). Jedes Bedürfnis reicht von 0 (verzweifelt) bis 100 (voll erfüllt). Verfallsraten sind persönlichkeitsbasiert – Extraverte verlieren Soziales schneller, gewissenhafte Agenten brauchen Bestimmung dringender.

trans-unit.source: Needs drive activity selection through a Utility AI system. When social need drops low, the agent is more likely to seek conversation. When purpose is urgent, they gravitate toward work. Activities fulfill specific needs: working fulfills purpose, socializing fulfills social, exploring fulfills stimulation.
trans-unit.target: Bedürfnisse treiben die Aktivitätswahl durch ein Utility-KI-System. Wenn das Sozialbedürfnis niedrig ist, sucht der Agent eher Gespräche. Wenn Bestimmung dringend ist, tendiert er zu Arbeit. Aktivitäten erfüllen bestimmte Bedürfnisse: Arbeiten erfüllt Bestimmung, Sozialisieren erfüllt Soziales, Erkunden erfüllt Stimulation.

trans-unit.source: Agents form opinions about each other (−100 to +100) through stacking modifiers. Sharing a zone builds familiarity (+3). Surviving a crisis together creates a bond (+15). Arguments reduce opinion (−12). When opinion crosses +60, a positive relationship is automatically created. When it drops below −60, hostility erupts.
trans-unit.target: Agenten bilden Meinungen übereinander (−100 bis +100) durch stapelbare Modifikatoren. Das Teilen einer Zone baut Vertrautheit auf (+3). Gemeinsames Überleben einer Krise schafft ein Band (+15). Streitigkeiten reduzieren die Meinung (−12). Wenn die Meinung +60 überschreitet, wird automatisch eine positive Beziehung erstellt. Unter −60 bricht Feindseligkeit aus.

trans-unit.source: Base compatibility is computed deterministically from personality profiles – agents with similar Big Five traits start with higher natural affinity. Opinion modifiers have three decay types (permanent, timed, decaying) and stacking caps to prevent runaway escalation.
trans-unit.target: Die Basiskompatibilität wird deterministisch aus Persönlichkeitsprofilen berechnet – Agenten mit ähnlichen Big-Five-Merkmalen starten mit höherer natürlicher Affinität. Meinungsmodifikatoren haben drei Verfallstypen (permanent, zeitlich begrenzt, abklingend) und Stapelungsgrenzen, um unkontrollierte Eskalation zu verhindern.

trans-unit.source: Each tick, agents choose an activity using Utility AI with Boltzmann selection. The system scores 14 possible activities (work, socialize, rest, explore, create, reflect, confront, celebrate, mourn, and more) based on current needs, personality traits, mood, and social context. The highest-scoring activity usually wins – but stressed agents make erratic choices.
trans-unit.target: Jeden Tick wählen Agenten eine Aktivität mittels Utility-KI mit Boltzmann-Selektion. Das System bewertet 14 mögliche Aktivitäten (arbeiten, sozialisieren, ruhen, erkunden, erschaffen, reflektieren, konfrontieren, feiern, trauern und mehr) basierend auf aktuellen Bedürfnissen, Persönlichkeitsmerkmalen, Stimmung und sozialem Kontext. Die höchstbewertete Aktivität gewinnt meistens – aber gestresste Agenten treffen erratische Entscheidungen.

trans-unit.source: Boltzmann selection uses a temperature parameter scaled by stress. At low stress, agents make rational choices (best utility wins). At high stress, temperature rises and the probability distribution flattens – agents may choose suboptimal or destructive activities. This creates emergent drama without scripted narratives.
trans-unit.target: Boltzmann-Selektion nutzt einen Temperaturparameter, der mit Stress skaliert. Bei niedrigem Stress treffen Agenten rationale Entscheidungen (beste Utility gewinnt). Bei hohem Stress steigt die Temperatur und die Wahrscheinlichkeitsverteilung flacht ab – Agenten wählen möglicherweise suboptimale oder destruktive Aktivitäten. Dies erzeugt emergentes Drama ohne geskriptete Erzählungen.

trans-unit.source: When two agents share a zone, they have a chance of interacting socially each tick. Interaction types include deep conversations (+8 opinion, +5 mood), casual chats (+3 opinion), insults (−15 opinion, only at low mood), comfort-seeking (+18 opinion from trusted friends), and confrontations (−20 opinion, can trigger conflict events).
trans-unit.target: Wenn zwei Agenten eine Zone teilen, haben sie jeden Tick eine Chance auf soziale Interaktion. Interaktionstypen umfassen tiefe Gespräche (+8 Meinung, +5 Stimmung), lockere Plauderei (+3 Meinung), Beleidigungen (−15 Meinung, nur bei schlechter Stimmung), Trostsuche (+18 Meinung von vertrauten Freunden) und Konfrontationen (−20 Meinung, kann Konfliktereignisse auslösen).

trans-unit.source: Interaction probability scales with sociability, existing relationships, and social need urgency. High-intensity relationships increase frequency. Interactions generate opinion modifiers, mood moodlets, and need fulfillment. Some interactions (insults, confrontations) can escalate into autonomous events visible in the timeline and chronicle.
trans-unit.target: Interaktionswahrscheinlichkeit skaliert mit Geselligkeit, bestehenden Beziehungen und Dringlichkeit des Sozialbedürfnisses. Hochintensive Beziehungen erhöhen die Häufigkeit. Interaktionen erzeugen Meinungsmodifikatoren, Stimmungs-Moodlets und Bedürfniserfüllung. Manche Interaktionen (Beleidigungen, Konfrontationen) können zu autonomen Ereignissen eskalieren, die in Zeitleiste und Chronik sichtbar sind.

trans-unit.source: When conditions align, the system generates autonomous events with AI narrative text. Six trigger types exist: stress breakdowns (agent snaps), relationship breakthroughs (deep bond forms), relationship breakdowns (hostility erupts), celebrations (3+ happy agents gather), zone crisis reactions (agents panic in unstable zones), and conflict escalations (arguments become public).
trans-unit.target: Wenn Bedingungen zusammentreffen, erzeugt das System autonome Ereignisse mit KI-generiertem Erzähltext. Sechs Auslösertypen existieren: Stress-Zusammenbrüche (Agent bricht zusammen), Beziehungsdurchbrüche (tiefes Band entsteht), Beziehungszusammenbrüche (Feindseligkeit bricht aus), Feierlichkeiten (3+ fröhliche Agenten versammeln sich), Zonenkrisenreaktionen (Agenten geraten in instabilen Zonen in Panik) und Konflikteskalationen (Streitigkeiten werden öffentlich).

trans-unit.source: Autonomous events are real entries in the events table – they participate in all standard flows: bleed threshold checks (high-impact events can echo to other simulations), chronicle generation (the AI newspaper reports on them), and zone stability impact. They also create moodlets for witness agents, potentially triggering cascade reactions.
trans-unit.target: Autonome Ereignisse sind reale Einträge in der Ereignistabelle – sie nehmen an allen Standardabläufen teil: Bleed-Schwellenwertprüfungen (schwerwiegende Ereignisse können in andere Simulationen echoieren), Chronik-Generierung (die KI-Zeitung berichtet darüber) und Zonenstabilitäts-Auswirkung. Sie erzeugen auch Moodlets für Zeugen-Agenten und lösen potenziell Kettenreaktionen aus.

trans-unit.source: Agent autonomy directly affects competitive epoch gameplay. Happy agents (mood > 50) get a +3% bonus to operative success probability. Distressed agents (mood < −50) suffer a −3% penalty. Agents under high stress (> 500) take an additional −3% hit. This means maintaining agent welfare is a strategic advantage – neglecting your population weakens your military operations.
trans-unit.target: Agentenautonomie beeinflusst direkt das kompetitive Epochen-Gameplay. Glückliche Agenten (Stimmung > 50) erhalten +3 % Bonus auf die Operativerfolgswahrscheinlichkeit. Verzweifelte Agenten (Stimmung < −50) erleiden −3 % Malus. Agenten unter hohem Stress (> 500) erhalten zusätzlich −3 %. Das bedeutet, das Wohlergehen der Agenten zu erhalten ist ein strategischer Vorteil – die Bevölkerung zu vernachlässigen schwächt die Militäroperationen.

trans-unit.source: A player who resolves conflicts, maintains buildings, and keeps zones stable will have happier, more effective operatives. This connects the sandbox (Living World) with competitive play (Epochs).
trans-unit.target: Ein Spieler, der Konflikte löst, Gebäude instand hält und Zonen stabil hält, wird glücklichere, effektivere Operative haben. Dies verbindet die Sandbox (Lebendige Welt) mit dem kompetitiven Spiel (Epochen).

trans-unit.source: The Daily Substrate Dispatch now includes an Agent Autonomy Report section. When you visit your simulation after agents have been active, you see: an AI-generated narrative summary in Bureau prose, a mood overview (happy/troubled/in crisis counts), significant activity highlights with priority classification, and relationship shifts since your last visit.
trans-unit.target: Die tägliche Substrat-Depesche enthält nun einen Abschnitt zum Agentenautonomiebericht. Wenn du deine Simulation besuchst, nachdem Agenten aktiv waren, siehst du: eine KI-generierte Erzählzusammenfassung im Bureau-Stil, einen Stimmungsüberblick (fröhlich/beunruhigt/in Krise), bedeutsame Aktivitätshighlights mit Prioritätsklassifizierung und Beziehungsveränderungen seit deinem letzten Besuch.

trans-unit.source: Agent autonomy is configured per simulation in Settings → Autonomy. The master toggle enables the Living World system. Below it, you can tune needs decay rate (how fast agents get restless), social interaction rate (how often they talk), event trigger sensitivity (how easily events fire), stress cascades (chain reactions), and the LLM budget per tick.
trans-unit.target: Agentenautonomie wird pro Simulation unter Einstellungen → Autonomie konfiguriert. Der Hauptschalter aktiviert das System der Lebendigen Welt. Darunter kannst du die Bedürfnisverfallsrate einstellen (wie schnell Agenten unruhig werden), die Sozialinteraktionsrate (wie oft sie reden), die Ereignisauslöse-Empfindlichkeit (wie leicht Ereignisse ausgelöst werden), Stresskaskaden (Kettenreaktionen) und das LLM-Budget pro Tick.

trans-unit.source: Activation requires either a personal OpenRouter API key (BYOK) or admin activation. When you provide your own key, AI narrative costs are charged to your account. When admin activates it, the platform covers costs. Rule-based mechanics (needs, mood, opinions, activities) always cost zero regardless.
trans-unit.target: Aktivierung erfordert entweder einen persönlichen OpenRouter-API-Schlüssel (BYOK) oder Admin-Aktivierung. Wenn du deinen eigenen Schlüssel verwendest, werden KI-Erzählkosten deinem Konto belastet. Bei Admin-Aktivierung trägt die Plattform die Kosten. Regelbasierte Mechaniken (Bedürfnisse, Stimmung, Meinungen, Aktivitäten) kosten immer null.

trans-unit.source: Each simulation is anchored to a real-world geographic location. Every heartbeat tick (4 hours), the platform fetches actual weather data from that location and transforms it into atmospheric zone events. Velgarien experiences Prague weather, Station Null gets Svalbard conditions, Speranza feels the Amalfi coast. The weather is real – if it rains in Prague right now, fog drifts through the Regierungsviertel.
trans-unit.target: Jede Simulation ist an einen realen geografischen Ort verankert. Bei jedem Heartbeat-Tick (4 Stunden) ruft die Plattform aktuelle Wetterdaten von diesem Ort ab und transformiert sie in atmosphärische Zonenereignisse. Velgarien erlebt Prager Wetter, Station Null bekommt Svalbard-Bedingungen, Speranza spürt die Amalfiküste. Das Wetter ist echt – wenn es gerade in Prag regnet, zieht Nebel durch das Regierungsviertel.

trans-unit.source: Weather conditions apply zone-ambient moodlets to every agent in the zone. Clear skies give +3 contentment. Thunderstorms cause -6 fear. Bitter cold (-10°C or below) adds -5 distress. Snow brings +1 wonder. These moodlets stack with the agent’s existing mood and personality, creating emergent behaviors: a neurotic agent in a thunderstorm may spiral toward a stress breakdown, while a resilient agent barely notices.
trans-unit.target: Wetterbedingungen wenden zonenweite Umgebungs-Moodlets auf jeden Agenten in der Zone an. Klarer Himmel gibt +3 Zufriedenheit. Gewitter verursachen −6 Angst. Bittere Kälte (−10 °C oder darunter) fügt −5 Verzweiflung hinzu. Schnee bringt +1 Staunen. Diese Moodlets stapeln sich mit der bestehenden Stimmung und Persönlichkeit des Agenten und erzeugen emergente Verhaltensweisen: Ein neurotischer Agent in einem Gewitter kann in einen Stress-Zusammenbruch spiralen, während ein resilienter Agent es kaum bemerkt.

trans-unit.source: Moodlet strength ranges from -8 (severe thunderstorm with hail) to +3 (clear sky). Each zone gets at most one weather moodlet at a time (stacking cap: 1). When the weather changes on the next tick, the old moodlet is replaced. Composite conditions (fog + cold, rain + wind) amplify the effect by 30%.
trans-unit.target: Die Moodlet-Stärke reicht von −8 (schweres Gewitter mit Hagel) bis +3 (klarer Himmel). Jede Zone erhält maximal ein Wetter-Moodlet gleichzeitig (Stapelungsgrenze: 1). Wenn sich das Wetter beim nächsten Tick ändert, wird das alte Moodlet ersetzt. Kombinationsbedingungen (Nebel + Kälte, Regen + Wind) verstärken den Effekt um 30 %.

trans-unit.source: Weather events appear as bilingual narrative entries in the heartbeat log and daily briefing. Each narrative is composed from four layers: an atmospheric opener (time of day), a core weather description, a theme-specific consequence, and an agent reaction based on the zone’s average mood. Over 1,000 unique template fragments per language combine into thousands of distinct descriptions that never repeat.
trans-unit.target: Wetterereignisse erscheinen als zweisprachige Erzähleinträge im Heartbeat-Log und täglichen Briefing. Jede Erzählung wird aus vier Schichten zusammengesetzt: eine atmosphärische Eröffnung (Tageszeit), eine Kernwetterbeschreibung, eine themenspezifische Konsequenz und eine Agentenreaktion basierend auf der Durchschnittsstimmung der Zone. Über 1.000 einzigartige Vorlagenfragmente pro Sprache kombinieren sich zu Tausenden verschiedener Beschreibungen, die sich nie wiederholen.

trans-unit.source: The system uses Tetris 7-bag anti-repetition: all template variants are shuffled into a bag and dealt sequentially. When the bag empties, it is reshuffled. Maximum drought before any variant repeats: 2N-1 draws. Combined with real weather data interpolation (actual temperature, wind speed, visibility), every narrative is effectively unique.
trans-unit.target: Das System nutzt Tetris-7-Bag-Anti-Wiederholung: Alle Vorlagenvarianten werden in einen Beutel gemischt und nacheinander ausgeteilt. Wenn der Beutel leer ist, wird er neu gemischt. Maximale Dürre bevor eine Variante sich wiederholt: 2N−1 Ziehungen. Kombiniert mit realer Wetterdateninterpolation (tatsächliche Temperatur, Windgeschwindigkeit, Sicht) ist jede Erzählung effektiv einzigartig.

trans-unit.source: Each main simulation is anchored to a location chosen for thematic resonance. Velgarien uses Prague – Cold War fog, gothic atmosphere. The Gaslit Reach uses Lofoten, Norway – dramatic Arctic storms. Station Null uses Svalbard – polar night and midnight sun. Speranza uses the Amalfi Coast – Mediterranean heat and storms. Cite des Dames uses Carcassonne – the Tramontane wind and medieval climate.
trans-unit.target: Jede Hauptsimulation ist an einen Ort verankert, der für thematische Resonanz gewählt wurde. Velgarien nutzt Prag – Kalter-Krieg-Nebel, gotische Atmosphäre. The Gaslit Reach nutzt Lofoten, Norwegen – dramatische arktische Stürme. Station Null nutzt Svalbard – Polarnacht und Mitternachtssonne. Speranza nutzt die Amalfiküste – Mittelmeerhitze und Stürme. Cité des Dames nutzt Carcassonne – der Tramontane-Wind und mittelalterliches Klima.

trans-unit.source: User-created simulations automatically receive coordinates matching their theme. You can override the coordinates in Settings → Weather to anchor your simulation anywhere in the world. Want your fantasy world to experience Tokyo weather? Set the coordinates to 35.68, 139.69.
trans-unit.target: Nutzererstellte Simulationen erhalten automatisch Koordinaten passend zu ihrem Thema. Du kannst die Koordinaten unter Einstellungen → Wetter überschreiben, um deine Simulation an jedem Ort der Welt zu verankern. Möchtest du, dass deine Fantasiewelt Tokioter Wetter erlebt? Setze die Koordinaten auf 35.68, 139.69.

trans-unit.source: The system calculates real moon phases using astronomical math (no API call needed). Full moons (illumination 45-55%) add -2 unease to agents – the Bureau reports increased activity. New moons (illumination below 5%) add +2 calm – ideal for covert operations. Moon phase is shown in the weather metadata and influences narrative tone.
trans-unit.target: Das System berechnet reale Mondphasen mittels astronomischer Mathematik (kein API-Aufruf nötig). Vollmonde (Beleuchtung 45–55 %) fügen −2 Unbehagen hinzu – das Bureau meldet erhöhte Aktivität. Neumonde (Beleuchtung unter 5 %) fügen +2 Ruhe hinzu – ideal für verdeckte Operationen. Die Mondphase wird in den Wetter-Metadaten angezeigt und beeinflusst den Erzählton.

trans-unit.source: Weather is configured per simulation in Settings → Weather. The master toggle enables weather seeding. Below it, you can set custom geographic coordinates (latitude and longitude) and override the narrative theme. By default, the narrative style matches your simulation’s theme – but you can mix: a medieval simulation with sci-fi weather descriptions, or a spy thriller with biopunk atmosphere.
trans-unit.target: Wetter wird pro Simulation unter Einstellungen → Wetter konfiguriert. Der Hauptschalter aktiviert das Wetter-Seeding. Darunter kannst du benutzerdefinierte geografische Koordinaten (Breiten- und Längengrad) festlegen und das Erzählthema überschreiben. Standardmäßig entspricht der Erzählstil dem Thema deiner Simulation – aber du kannst mischen: eine mittelalterliche Simulation mit Sci-Fi-Wetterbeschreibungen oder ein Spionagethriller mit Biopunk-Atmosphäre.

trans-unit.source: API KEY ACTIVE – NO TOKEN DEDUCTION
When enabled, agents act autonomously between player sessions: forming opinions, pursuing activities, reacting to events, and generating social dynamics. The world lives while you are away.
trans-unit.target: Wenn aktiviert, handeln Agenten zwischen Spielersitzungen autonom: Sie bilden Meinungen, verfolgen Aktivitäten, reagieren auf Ereignisse und erzeugen soziale Dynamiken. Die Welt lebt, während du weg bist.

trans-unit.source: Autonomy has been activated by the platform admin. AI narrative costs are covered by the platform. You can toggle it on or off for this simulation.
trans-unit.target: Autonomie wurde vom Plattformadministrator aktiviert. KI-Erzählkosten werden von der Plattform getragen. Du kannst sie für diese Simulation ein- oder ausschalten.

trans-unit.source: You are using your personal OpenRouter API key to power agent autonomy. AI narrative generation (autonomous events, morning briefings) will use your key. Rule-based mechanics (needs, mood, opinions, activity selection) have zero AI cost.
trans-unit.target: Du nutzt deinen persönlichen OpenRouter-API-Schlüssel für die Agentenautonomie. KI-Erzählgenerierung (autonome Ereignisse, Morgenbriefings) verwendet deinen Schlüssel. Regelbasierte Mechaniken (Bedürfnisse, Stimmung, Meinungen, Aktivitätswahl) haben null KI-Kosten.

trans-unit.source: AI costs are covered by the platform admin. No personal API key required.
trans-unit.target: KI-Kosten werden vom Plattformadministrator getragen. Kein persönlicher API-Schlüssel erforderlich.

trans-unit.source: Agent autonomy requires an OpenRouter API key to power AI narrative generation (autonomous events, morning briefings). You can add your personal key in your profile settings under "API Keys". Rule-based mechanics have zero AI cost, but the feature needs a key to be activated.
trans-unit.target: Agentenautonomie benötigt einen OpenRouter-API-Schlüssel für die KI-Erzählgenerierung (autonome Ereignisse, Morgenbriefings). Du kannst deinen persönlichen Schlüssel in deinen Profileinstellungen unter "API-Schlüssel" hinzufügen. Regelbasierte Mechaniken haben null KI-Kosten, aber die Funktion benötigt einen Schlüssel zur Aktivierung.

trans-unit.source: The platform admin can also activate autonomy globally, which covers AI costs with the platform key. Contact your admin to request activation for this simulation.
trans-unit.target: Der Plattformadministrator kann Autonomie auch global aktivieren, wodurch die Plattform die KI-Kosten mit dem Plattform-Schlüssel trägt. Kontaktiere deinen Admin, um die Aktivierung für diese Simulation anzufordern.

trans-unit.source: Agent autonomy is disabled for this simulation. Toggle the switch above to activate the Living World system.
trans-unit.target: Agentenautonomie ist für diese Simulation deaktiviert. Schalte den Schalter oben um, um das System der Lebendigen Welt zu aktivieren.

trans-unit.source: Multiplier for how quickly agent needs (social, purpose, safety, comfort, stimulation) decrease each tick. At 1.0: standard rate. At 2.0: agents get restless twice as fast, creating more activity. At 0.5: agents are more content, fewer urgent decisions. Range: 0.1 to 3.0.
trans-unit.target: Multiplikator für die Geschwindigkeit, mit der Agentenbedürfnisse (sozial, Bestimmung, Sicherheit, Komfort, Stimulation) jeden Tick abnehmen. Bei 1,0: Standardrate. Bei 2,0: Agenten werden doppelt so schnell unruhig, mehr Aktivität. Bei 0,5: Agenten sind zufriedener, weniger dringende Entscheidungen. Bereich: 0,1 bis 3,0.

trans-unit.source: Multiplier for how often co-located agents interact socially. At 1.0: ~15% chance per agent pair per tick. At 2.0: ~30% chance, very socially active. At 0.5: quieter simulation with fewer conflicts and friendships. Range: 0.1 to 3.0.
trans-unit.target: Multiplikator für die Häufigkeit sozialer Interaktionen zwischen Agenten am gleichen Ort. Bei 1,0: ~15 % Chance pro Agentenpaar pro Tick. Bei 2,0: ~30 % Chance, sehr sozial aktiv. Bei 0,5: ruhigere Simulation mit weniger Konflikten und Freundschaften. Bereich: 0,1 bis 3,0.

trans-unit.source: Controls how easily autonomous events fire. At 0.5 (default): standard sensitivity. At 1.0: events trigger at the slightest provocation, creating a dramatic, chaotic simulation. At 0.1: only extreme conditions generate events. Affects celebrations, crises, and conflict escalations. Range: 0.1 to 1.0.
trans-unit.target: Steuert, wie leicht autonome Ereignisse ausgelöst werden. Bei 0,5 (Standard): normale Empfindlichkeit. Bei 1,0: Ereignisse feuern bei geringstem Anlass, was eine dramatische, chaotische Simulation erzeugt. Bei 0,1: nur extreme Bedingungen generieren Ereignisse. Betrifft Feiern, Krisen und Konflikteskalationen. Bereich: 0,1 bis 1,0.

trans-unit.source: When an agent has a stress breakdown (stress > 800), all agents in the same zone receive a negative moodlet. This can trigger chain reactions where one breakdown destabilizes an entire zone. Inspired by Dwarf Fortress tantrum spirals. Disable for a calmer simulation.
trans-unit.target: Wenn ein Agent einen Stress-Zusammenbruch hat (Stress > 800), erhalten alle Agenten in derselben Zone ein negatives Moodlet. Dies kann Kettenreaktionen auslösen, bei denen ein Zusammenbruch eine ganze Zone destabilisiert. Inspiriert von Dwarf-Fortress-Wutspiralen. Deaktivieren für eine ruhigere Simulation.

trans-unit.source: When an agent's opinion of another crosses +60, a positive relationship (ally/friend) is automatically created. When it crosses -60, a hostile relationship (rival) is created or an existing one is downgraded. Disable to keep manual control over all relationships.
trans-unit.target: Wenn die Meinung eines Agenten über einen anderen +60 überschreitet, wird automatisch eine positive Beziehung (Verbündeter/Freund) erstellt. Unter −60 wird eine feindliche Beziehung (Rivale) erstellt oder eine bestehende herabgestuft. Deaktivieren für manuelle Kontrolle über alle Beziehungen.

trans-unit.source: Maximum number of AI text generation calls per heartbeat tick for this simulation. Used for autonomous event narratives and morning briefing prose. Higher values produce richer narratives but cost more. Activity selection and mood/opinion calculations use zero LLM calls. Range: 1 to 20.
trans-unit.target: Maximale Anzahl KI-Textgenerierungsaufrufe pro Heartbeat-Tick für diese Simulation. Wird für autonome Ereigniserzählungen und Morgenbriefing-Prosa verwendet. Höhere Werte erzeugen reichere Erzählungen, kosten aber mehr. Aktivitätswahl und Stimmungs-/Meinungsberechnungen nutzen null LLM-Aufrufe. Bereich: 1 bis 20.

trans-unit.source: How morning briefings are generated. "Narrative" mode uses AI to write a Bureau-style prose summary of overnight activity (costs 1 LLM call). "Data" mode shows only structured metrics without prose. Choose data mode to save on AI costs.
trans-unit.target: Wie Morgenbriefings generiert werden. "Erzählung" nutzt KI für eine Bureau-Stil-Prosazusammenfassung nächtlicher Aktivität (kostet 1 LLM-Aufruf). "Daten" zeigt nur strukturierte Metriken ohne Prosa. Wähle Datenmodus, um KI-Kosten zu sparen.

trans-unit.source: Real-world weather conditions influence zone atmosphere and agent mood. Each simulation is anchored to a geographic location matching its theme. Weather data is fetched every heartbeat tick (4 hours) from the Open-Meteo API.
trans-unit.target: Echtzeit-Wetterbedingungen beeinflussen die Zonenatmosphäre und Agentenstimmung. Jede Simulation ist an einen geografischen Ort verankert, der zu ihrem Thema passt. Wetterdaten werden bei jedem Heartbeat-Tick (4 Stunden) von der Open-Meteo-API abgerufen.

trans-unit.source: When enabled, real-world weather at the simulation's geographic anchor generates ambient zone events and applies mood effects to agents. Zero AI cost -- all narratives are template-based. One API call per tick.
trans-unit.target: Wenn aktiviert, erzeugt Echtzeit-Wetter am geografischen Anker der Simulation atmosphärische Zonenereignisse und Stimmungseffekte bei Agenten. Null KI-Kosten – alle Erzählungen sind vorlagenbasiert. Ein API-Aufruf pro Tick.

trans-unit.source: Weather seeding is disabled. Enable it to let real-world conditions influence your simulation's atmosphere.
trans-unit.target: Wetter-Seeding ist deaktiviert. Aktiviere es, um reale Bedingungen die Atmosphäre deiner Simulation beeinflussen zu lassen.

trans-unit.source: The real-world location used for weather data. Leave empty to use the default for your simulation's theme.
trans-unit.target: Der reale Ort für Wetterdaten. Leer lassen für den Standard deines Simulationsthemas.

trans-unit.source: Latitude and longitude of the real-world location. Examples: Prague (50.08, 14.44), Svalbard (78.22, 15.63), Amalfi (40.63, 14.60), Carcassonne (43.21, 2.35). Leave blank to use the theme default.
trans-unit.target: Breiten- und Längengrad des realen Ortes. Beispiele: Prag (50.08, 14.44), Svalbard (78.22, 15.63), Amalfi (40.63, 14.60), Carcassonne (43.21, 2.35). Leer lassen für den Themen-Standard.

trans-unit.source: Override the narrative tone of weather descriptions. By default, your simulation's theme determines the style (spy-thriller gets surveillance metaphors, sci-fi gets system alerts, etc.). Override only if you want a different atmosphere.
trans-unit.target: Überschreibe den Erzählton von Wetterbeschreibungen. Standardmäßig bestimmt das Thema deiner Simulation den Stil (Spionagethriller bekommt Überwachungsmetaphern, Sci-Fi bekommt Systemwarnungen usw.). Nur überschreiben, wenn du eine andere Atmosphäre möchtest.

trans-unit.source: Spy Thriller -- surveillance, curfews, dead drops
Sci-Fi -- system alerts, hull sensors, protocols
No zones found in this simulation.
trans-unit.target: Keine Zonen in dieser Simulation gefunden.

trans-unit.source: Failed to load page. Please refresh.
trans-unit.target: Seite konnte nicht geladen werden. Bitte aktualisieren.

trans-unit.source: is not assigned to any building.
Insufficient clearance for ceremony initiation. Architect access required.
trans-unit.target: Unzureichende Freigabestufe für Zeremonieeinleitung. Architekt-Zugang erforderlich.

trans-unit.source: Start a conversation with an agent
Fortify a zone (-15% event pressure, 7 days)
Quarantine a zone (14 days, 2 ops points)
trans-unit.target: Zone unter Quarantäne stellen (14 Tage, 2 Ops-Punkte)

trans-unit.source: Events cannot spread to/from zone for 14 days. Agents locked in place.
trans-unit.target: Ereignisse können 14 Tage lang nicht in/aus der Zone gelangen. Agenten vor Ort gesperrt.

trans-unit.source: You are a Bureau field operative. Observe zones,
trans-unit.target: Du bist ein Bureau-Feldoperativ. Beobachte Zonen,

trans-unit.source: interrogate agents, and manage field operations.
This terminal provides local intelligence that
trans-unit.target: Dieses Terminal liefert lokale Aufklärung, die
Tippe 'look' um deine Umgebung zu beobachten.

trans-unit.source: Hint: Use 'examine {agent name}' to access a dossier.
Hint: Use 'talk {agent name}' to initiate contact.
Hint: Use 'go {zone name}' to move to another sector.
trans-unit.target: Hinweis: Benutze 'go {Zonenname}' um in einen anderen Sektor zu wechseln.

trans-unit.source: Hint: Use 'status' for a full situation report.
trans-unit.target: Hinweis: Benutze 'status' für einen vollständigen Lagebericht.

trans-unit.source: Onboarding complete. You have full LEVEL 1 clearance.
trans-unit.target: Einweisung abgeschlossen. Du hast volle STUFE-1-Freigabe.

trans-unit.source: Directional navigation is not available. Use 'go {zone name}' or type 'map' to see available zones.
trans-unit.target: Richtungsnavigation ist nicht verfügbar. Benutze 'go {Zonenname}' oder tippe 'map' um verfügbare Zonen zu sehen.

trans-unit.source: Radar sweep of all sectors (1 intel point)
Deep investigation of an event (1 intel point)
Formal agent debrief (1 intel point, AI)
Ask agent about a specific topic (AI)
trans-unit.target: Agenten zu einem bestimmten Thema befragen (KI)

trans-unit.source: A text-based command interface that places you inside the simulation. Type commands, interrogate agents, gather intelligence, and manage zone operations through narrative prose. Three clearance tiers unlock progressively as you gain experience: Observation, Field Operations, and Intelligence Network.
trans-unit.target: Eine textbasierte Befehlsschnittstelle, die Sie direkt in die Simulation versetzt. Geben Sie Befehle ein, verhören Sie Agenten, sammeln Sie Geheimdienstinformationen und verwalten Sie Zonenoperationen durch narrative Prosa. Drei Freigabestufen werden progressiv mit wachsender Erfahrung freigeschaltet: Observation, Feldoperationen und Geheimdienstnetzwerk.

trans-unit.source: Active platform resonances generate subtle mood effects on agents. Each of the 8 Jungian archetypes maps to a specific emotional pressure: The Shadow (conflict) creates anxiety, The Prometheus (innovation) inspires hope, The Entropy (environmental decay) breeds despair. These background moodlets range from −2 to +2 – atmospheric pressure, not a dominant force.
trans-unit.target: Aktive Plattform-Resonanzen erzeugen subtile Stimmungseffekte bei Agenten. Jeder der 8 Jungschen Archetypen erzeugt einen spezifischen emotionalen Druck: The Shadow (Konflikt) erzeugt Angst, The Prometheus (Innovation) weckt Hoffnung, The Entropy (Umweltverfall) nährt Verzweiflung. Diese Hintergrund-Moodlets bewegen sich im Bereich von −2 bis +2 – atmosphärischer Druck, keine dominierende Kraft.

trans-unit.source: Resonance moodlets are applied atomically each heartbeat tick: all previous resonance moodlets are deleted and fresh ones inserted based on current active resonances. Subsiding resonances contribute at half strength. The effect scales with your simulation’s susceptibility profile – a simulation highly susceptible to conflict archetypes feels The Shadow more intensely.
trans-unit.target: Resonanz-Moodlets werden bei jedem Heartbeat-Tick atomar angewendet: Alle vorherigen Resonanz-Moodlets werden gelöscht und neue basierend auf den aktuell aktiven Resonanzen eingefügt. Abklingende Resonanzen wirken mit halber Stärke. Der Effekt skaliert mit dem Empfindlichkeitsprofil Ihrer Simulation – eine Simulation mit hoher Anfälligkeit für Konflikt-Archetypen spürt The Shadow intensiver.

trans-unit.source: The Bureau Terminal is a text-based command interface for interacting with simulations. Instead of clicking through dashboards, you type commands and receive narrative prose responses. The terminal places you inside the world with a local perspective – you can only see what is in your current zone. Information beyond your position must be actively gathered through reconnaissance and agent interrogation.
trans-unit.target: Das Bureau Terminal ist eine textbasierte Befehlsschnittstelle zur Interaktion mit Simulationen. Statt durch Dashboards zu klicken, geben Sie Befehle ein und erhalten narrative Prosa-Antworten. Das Terminal versetzt Sie mit lokaler Perspektive in die Welt – Sie sehen nur, was sich in Ihrer aktuellen Zone befindet. Informationen jenseits Ihrer Position müssen aktiv durch Aufklärung und Agenten-Verhöre beschafft werden.

trans-unit.source: Access the terminal from any simulation via the Terminal tab. It features a full CRT aesthetic with scanlines, phosphor glow, and chromatic aberration. A boot sequence plays on first entry. Commands are parsed with fuzzy matching (Levenshtein distance) so minor typos are corrected automatically.
trans-unit.target: Zugriff auf das Terminal erfolgt über den Terminal-Tab jeder Simulation. Es bietet eine vollständige CRT-Ästhetik mit Scanlines, Phosphor-Leuchten und chromatischer Aberration. Beim ersten Zugang wird eine Bootsequenz abgespielt. Befehle werden mit Fuzzy-Matching (Levenshtein-Distanz) verarbeitet, sodass kleinere Tippfehler automatisch korrigiert werden.

trans-unit.source: Available immediately. Navigate the simulation with look (inspect current zone), go (move between zones), examine (inspect agents or buildings), talk (open AI conversation with an agent), weather (real-time conditions), status (full situation report), map (ASCII zone overview), where (current position), and help (command reference).
trans-unit.target: Sofort verfügbar. Navigieren Sie die Simulation mit look (aktuelle Zone inspizieren), go (zwischen Zonen wechseln), examine (Agenten oder Gebäude untersuchen), talk (KI-Gespräch mit einem Agenten eröffnen), weather (Echtzeit-Bedingungen), status (vollständiger Lagebericht), map (ASCII-Zonenübersicht), where (aktuelle Position) und help (Befehlsreferenz).

trans-unit.source: The look command shows zone stability, security level, weather, buildings with readiness percentages, agents present, active events, and exits to adjacent zones. The examine command produces detailed agent dossiers including influence tier, mood, stress, needs radar, active moodlets, and relationship summary.
trans-unit.target: Der Befehl look zeigt Zonenstabilität, Sicherheitsstufe, Wetter, Gebäude mit Bereitschaftsprozenten, anwesende Agenten, aktive Ereignisse und Ausgänge zu angrenzenden Zonen. Der Befehl examine erstellt detaillierte Agenten-Dossiers einschließlich Einflussstufe, Stimmung, Stress, Bedürfnisradar, aktive Moodlets und Beziehungsübersicht.

trans-unit.source: Zone overview with stability, agents, events
Move to adjacent zone by name or direction
trans-unit.target: Zur angrenzenden Zone nach Name oder Richtung wechseln

trans-unit.source: AI conversation mode with an agent

Unlocks at 10 total commands. You become a field commander with the ability to act, not just observe. Fortify a zone (−15% event pressure for 7 days), quarantine a zone (block event spread for 14 days), assign or unassign agents to buildings, and trigger ceremonies.
trans-unit.target: Freischaltung bei 10 ausgeführten Befehlen. Sie werden zum Feldkommandeur mit der Fähigkeit zu handeln, nicht nur zu beobachten. Verstärken Sie eine Zone (−15 % Ereignisdruck für 7 Tage), verhängen Sie Quarantäne über eine Zone (Ereignisausbreitung blockieren für 14 Tage), weisen Sie Agenten Gebäuden zu oder ab, und lösen Sie Zeremonien aus.

trans-unit.source: Field operations consume Operations Points (3 per heartbeat cycle). Fortify costs 1 point, quarantine costs 2. You cannot fortify every zone and act everywhere – you must prioritize. Quick action buttons appear below the terminal for common commands.
trans-unit.target: Feldoperationen verbrauchen Operations Points (3 pro Heartbeat-Zyklus). Verstärkung kostet 1 Punkt, Quarantäne kostet 2. Sie können nicht jede Zone verstärken und überall agieren – Sie müssen Prioritäten setzen. Schnellaktionstasten erscheinen unterhalb des Terminals für häufige Befehle.

trans-unit.source: Unlocks at 25 total commands. The terminal transforms from an observation post into an intelligence workstation. Five new commands let you gather structured data: scan (all-zone radar sweep), investigate (event deep dive), report (session summary document), debrief (formal AI-powered agent report), and ask (targeted AI query to any agent).
trans-unit.target: Freischaltung bei 25 ausgeführten Befehlen. Das Terminal wandelt sich von einem Beobachtungsposten zu einer Geheimdienstzentrale. Fünf neue Befehle ermöglichen strukturierte Datenerhebung: scan (Radar-Rundumerfassung aller Zonen), investigate (Ereignis-Tiefenanalyse), report (Sitzungszusammenfassung), debrief (formeller KI-gestützter Agentenbericht) und ask (gezielte KI-Anfrage an jeden Agenten).

trans-unit.source: Scan, investigate, and debrief each cost 1 Intel Point (2 per cycle). The debrief command produces a three-section Bureau report: Zone Assessment, Personnel Note, and Recommendation. Two agents debriefed about the same situation may give contradictory accounts – deciding who to trust is the gameplay. The ask command is free but limited by agent knowledge scope.
trans-unit.target: Scan, investigate und debrief kosten jeweils 1 Intel Point (2 pro Zyklus). Der Befehl debrief erstellt einen dreiteiligen Bureau-Bericht: Zonenbewertung, Personalvermerk und Empfehlung. Zwei Agenten, die zur selben Lage befragt werden, können widersprüchliche Aussagen liefern – die Entscheidung, wem Sie vertrauen, ist das Spielprinzip. Der Befehl ask ist kostenlos, aber durch den Wissenshorizont des Agenten begrenzt.

trans-unit.source: Operations Points and Intel Points refresh each heartbeat cycle but cannot be stockpiled. You cannot fortify every zone AND debrief every agent. You must choose between acting and learning. This creates the core strategic tension: do you reinforce a failing zone, or interrogate agents to understand why it is failing? Do you spend intel on a radar sweep or save it for a targeted investigation?
trans-unit.target: Operations Points und Intel Points werden jeden Heartbeat-Zyklus aufgefrischt, können aber nicht angespart werden. Sie können nicht jede Zone verstärken UND jeden Agenten befragen. Sie müssen zwischen Handeln und Erkenntnisgewinn wählen. Dies erzeugt die strategische Kernspannung: Verstärken Sie eine versagende Zone, oder verhören Sie Agenten, um zu verstehen, warum sie versagt? Investieren Sie Intel in eine Radar-Erfassung oder sparen Sie es für eine gezielte Untersuchung?

trans-unit.source: Agents only know about their own zone, their building, and agents they have relationships with. A general in the military academy knows different things than an inspector in the government quarter. Triangulate information from multiple sources to build the full picture.
trans-unit.target: Agenten wissen nur über ihre eigene Zone, ihr Gebäude und Agenten Bescheid, zu denen sie Beziehungen unterhalten. Ein General in der Militärakademie kennt andere Dinge als ein Inspektor im Regierungsviertel. Triangulieren Sie Informationen aus mehreren Quellen, um das vollständige Lagebild zu erstellen.

trans-unit.source: Foreign operative detected in this zone. Identity unknown.
trans-unit.target: Feindlicher Operativ in dieser Zone erfasst. Identität unbekannt.

trans-unit.source: Use 'intercept' to attempt identification and capture.
Recommend spy deployment via Operations Console.
Threat detection requires OPERATIONAL MODE (epoch context).
No incoming threats detected. Sector clear.
trans-unit.target: Keine eingehenden Bedrohungen erkannt. Sektor gesichert.

trans-unit.source: No foreign operatives found in sector.
trans-unit.target: Keine feindlichen Operative im Sektor gefunden.

trans-unit.source: RP refreshes each cycle. Plan operations carefully.
trans-unit.target: RP werden jeden Zyklus aufgefrischt. Plane Einsätze mit Bedacht.

trans-unit.source: Browse 12 topics covering every game system in metaverse.center.
trans-unit.target: Browse 12 topics covering every game system in metaverse.center.

trans-unit.source: 12 topics covering every system. Pick a dossier.
trans-unit.target: 12 Themen zu jedem Spielsystem. Wähle ein Dossier.

trans-unit.source: Learn how to play metaverse.center: quick start guide, game mechanics reference, and competitive strategy resources.
trans-unit.target: Learn how to play metaverse.center: quick start guide, game mechanics reference, and competitive strategy resources.

trans-unit.source: Everything you need to know in 5 minutes. What is this place, how to explore, and what to do first.
trans-unit.target: Alles was du in 5 Minuten wissen musst. Was ist dieser Ort, wie erkundet man ihn, und was tut man zuerst.

trans-unit.source: 12 topic pages covering every system: agents, events, epochs, operatives, scoring, alliances, and more.
trans-unit.target: 12 Themenseiten zu jedem System: Agenten, Ereignisse, Epochen, Operative, Wertung, Allianzen und mehr.

trans-unit.source: Competitive tactics, worked-out match replays, 200-game balance analytics, and the meta-strategy tier list.
trans-unit.target: Kompetitive Taktiken, ausgearbeitete Partiewiederholungen, Balance-Analysen aus 200 Partien und die Meta-Strategie-Rangliste.

trans-unit.source: Three paths into the simulation. Choose what fits your needs.
trans-unit.target: Drei Wege in die Simulation. Wähle, was zu dir passt.

trans-unit.source: Get started with metaverse.center in 5 minutes. Create AI-driven worlds, chat with agents, and compete in epochs.
trans-unit.target: Get started with metaverse.center in 5 minutes. Create AI-driven worlds, chat with agents, and compete in epochs.

trans-unit.source: AI-driven simulations – fictional worlds where agents have personalities, memories, and opinions. Everything is generated: dialogue, newspapers, weather reports, even the lore. Each world is alive.
trans-unit.target: AI-driven simulations – fictional worlds where agents have personalities, memories, and opinions. Everything is generated: dialogue, newspapers, weather reports, even the lore. Each world is alive.

trans-unit.source: The Simulation Forge turns a single seed idea into a complete world – geography, agents, buildings, lore, and a visual identity – in about 15 minutes. No coding, no setup.
trans-unit.target: The Simulation Forge turns a single seed idea into a complete world – geography, agents, buildings, lore, and a visual identity – in about 15 minutes. No coding, no setup.

trans-unit.source: Every agent remembers your conversations. They reference past events, form opinions about you, and develop richer personalities over time. Chat is persistent across sessions.
trans-unit.target: Every agent remembers your conversations. They reference past events, form opinions about you, and develop richer personalities over time. Chat is persistent across sessions.

trans-unit.source: Epochs are time-limited PvP seasons scored across five dimensions. Deploy operatives, forge alliances, sabotage rivals. Your original world is never modified – gameplay happens on a balanced clone.
trans-unit.target: Epochs are time-limited PvP seasons scored across five dimensions. Deploy operatives, forge alliances, sabotage rivals. Your original world is never modified – gameplay happens on a balanced clone.

trans-unit.source: Five things you can do right now:
trans-unit.target: Fünf Dinge, die du jetzt sofort tun kannst:

trans-unit.source: Browse any simulation – no login required
Read the lore – every world has a unique story
trans-unit.target: Die Überlieferung lesen – jede Welt hat eine einzigartige Geschichte

trans-unit.source: Chat with an agent – they will remember you
trans-unit.target: Mit einem Agenten chatten – sie werden sich an dich erinnern

trans-unit.source: Create your own world with the Forge
trans-unit.target: Erstelle deine eigene Welt mit der Schmiede

trans-unit.source: Join an Epoch when you are ready to compete
trans-unit.target: Tritt einer Epoche bei, wenn du bereit bist zu kämpfen

trans-unit.source: Everything you need to know in 5 minutes.
trans-unit.target: Alles was du in 5 Minuten wissen musst.

trans-unit.source: Ready for more? Browse the Game Guide
trans-unit.target: Bereit für mehr? Den Spielleitfaden durchstöbern

trans-unit.source: This topic does not exist in the game guide.
trans-unit.target: Dieses Thema existiert nicht im Spielleitfaden.

trans-unit.source: Competitive tactics, worked-out match replays, and 200-game balance analytics for metaverse.center epochs.
trans-unit.target: Competitive tactics, worked-out match replays, and 200-game balance analytics for metaverse.center epochs.

trans-unit.source: Navigate to the Forge from the main navigation. The Forge is the Bureau of Impossible Geography – a four-phase wizard that materializes an entirely new simulation through AI-assisted procedural generation. Each phase builds on the previous, culminating in a fully realized world with geography, agents, buildings, lore, and a unique visual identity.
trans-unit.target: Navigate to the Forge from the main navigation. The Forge is the Bureau of Impossible Geography – a four-phase wizard that materializes an entirely new simulation through AI-assisted procedural generation. Each phase builds on the previous, culminating in a fully realized world with geography, agents, buildings, lore, and a unique visual identity.

trans-unit.source: The Forge uses real AI generation at every step. Each scan, draft, and calibration is a live API call – results are unique every time. Budget 10–15 minutes for a full forge run.
trans-unit.target: The Forge uses real AI generation at every step. Each scan, draft, and calibration is a live API call – results are unique every time. Budget 10–15 minutes for a full forge run.

trans-unit.source: Enter a seed prompt describing the world you want to create. This can be as simple as "a cyberpunk megacity" or as evocative as "a floating archipelago where memories solidify into islands." Click Scan Multiverse to generate three philosophical anchor cards – each offers a distinct thematic interpretation of your seed.
trans-unit.target: Enter a seed prompt describing the world you want to create. This can be as simple as "a cyberpunk megacity" or as evocative as "a floating archipelago where memories solidify into islands." Click Scan Multiverse to generate three philosophical anchor cards – each offers a distinct thematic interpretation of your seed.

trans-unit.source: The AI analyzes your seed and produces three anchors with names, philosophical tenets, and worldbuilding implications. Each anchor shapes the entire simulation differently – the same seed with different anchors creates radically different worlds.
trans-unit.target: The AI analyzes your seed and produces three anchors with names, philosophical tenets, and worldbuilding implications. Each anchor shapes the entire simulation differently – the same seed with different anchors creates radically different worlds.

trans-unit.source: A live preview panel on the right shows how your theme will look in practice – color swatches, sample headings, buttons, and a mock agent card. Every parameter is editable if you want to fine-tune the AI’s choices.
trans-unit.target: A live preview panel on the right shows how your theme will look in practice – color swatches, sample headings, buttons, and a mock agent card. Every parameter is editable if you want to fine-tune the AI’s choices.

trans-unit.source: The Substrate Pulse is the heartbeat of every simulation. Every 4 hours, the system ticks – updating zone stability, progressing narrative arcs, triggering bureau responses, and recording entries in the pulse chronicle. The Pulse tab shows this activity as a battle-log-style ticker, grouped by tick number, with color-coded severity and type indicators.
trans-unit.target: The Substrate Pulse is the heartbeat of every simulation. Every 4 hours, the system ticks – updating zone stability, progressing narrative arcs, triggering bureau responses, and recording entries in the pulse chronicle. The Pulse tab shows this activity as a battle-log-style ticker, grouped by tick number, with color-coded severity and type indicators.

trans-unit.source: When you visit a simulation for the first time each day, a classified intelligence briefing appears – the Daily Substrate Dispatch. Styled as a Bureau report handed to an operative, it summarizes the last 24 hours: overall health status with a color-coded bar, counts of critical and positive events, total pulse entries, and active narrative arcs with pressure indicators.
trans-unit.target: When you visit a simulation for the first time each day, a classified intelligence briefing appears – the Daily Substrate Dispatch. Styled as a Bureau report handed to an operative, it summarizes the last 24 hours: overall health status with a color-coded bar, counts of critical and positive events, total pulse entries, and active narrative arcs with pressure indicators.

trans-unit.source: The dispatch auto-dismisses after 30 seconds (pause by hovering) or can be acknowledged immediately. Click "View Chronicle" to jump straight to the Pulse tab. The briefing only appears when there are new entries to report – if nothing happened in 24 hours, no briefing is shown.
trans-unit.target: The dispatch auto-dismisses after 30 seconds (pause by hovering) or can be acknowledged immediately. Click "View Chronicle" to jump straight to the Pulse tab. The briefing only appears when there are new entries to report – if nothing happened in 24 hours, no briefing is shown.

trans-unit.source: The Chronicle is a living newspaper generated by AI after each epoch cycle. Styled as a Victorian-era broadsheet, it reports on in-game events through the lens of fictional journalism – complete with dramatic headlines, editorial commentary, and intelligence dispatches.
trans-unit.target: The Chronicle is a living newspaper generated by AI after each epoch cycle. Styled as a Victorian-era broadsheet, it reports on in-game events through the lens of fictional journalism – complete with dramatic headlines, editorial commentary, and intelligence dispatches.

trans-unit.source: The Chronicle is publicly visible – anyone browsing the simulation can read it. Use it to understand the narrative arc of an epoch, even as a spectator.
trans-unit.target: The Chronicle is publicly visible – anyone browsing the simulation can read it. Use it to understand the narrative arc of an epoch, even as a spectator.

trans-unit.source: Substrate Resonances are emergent phenomena that arise when a simulation’s substrate – its underlying computational fabric – vibrates in response to in-game activity. Think of them as the world’s immune system: when events cluster too densely or score dimensions become imbalanced, the substrate begins to resonate.
trans-unit.target: Substrate Resonances are emergent phenomena that arise when a simulation’s substrate – its underlying computational fabric – vibrates in response to in-game activity. Think of them as the world’s immune system: when events cluster too densely or score dimensions become imbalanced, the substrate begins to resonate.

trans-unit.source: The Substrate Tremor Monitor displays all active resonances in real time. Each resonance is shown as a card with its signature label, magnitude bar, and remaining decay cycles. The monitor also shows the simulation’s overall substrate stability – a composite measure of how far the world’s fabric has been stressed.
trans-unit.target: The Substrate Tremor Monitor displays all active resonances in real time. Each resonance is shown as a card with its signature label, magnitude bar, and remaining decay cycles. The monitor also shows the simulation’s overall substrate stability – a composite measure of how far the world’s fabric has been stressed.

trans-unit.source: High substrate instability means resonances are more likely to trigger and cascade. Plan your deployments carefully during unstable periods – a single saboteur mission could trigger a resonance that rebounds against you.
trans-unit.target: High substrate instability means resonances are more likely to trigger and cascade. Plan your deployments carefully during unstable periods – a single saboteur mission could trigger a resonance that rebounds against you.

trans-unit.source: Resonances are not just atmospheric – they have direct mechanical impact. A "Military Surge" resonance might increase assassin success rates by 10% for 3 cycles, while a "Diplomatic Dampener" could reduce embassy effectiveness. Understanding which actions trigger which resonances is key to advanced strategy.
trans-unit.target: Resonances are not just atmospheric – they have direct mechanical impact. A "Military Surge" resonance might increase assassin success rates by 10% for 3 cycles, while a "Diplomatic Dampener" could reduce embassy effectiveness. Understanding which actions trigger which resonances is key to advanced strategy.

trans-unit.source: Every simulation tracks event pressure across its zones through the Event Seismograph – an SVG visualization that plots event intensity over time. The seismograph shows spikes when missions resolve, guardians deploy, or bleed events cascade. Hover over any point to see the exact event details.
trans-unit.target: Every simulation tracks event pressure across its zones through the Event Seismograph – an SVG visualization that plots event intensity over time. The seismograph shows spikes when missions resolve, guardians deploy, or bleed events cascade. Hover over any point to see the exact event details.

trans-unit.source: Zone security is the primary defensive mechanic. A zone at Critical security reduces enemy operative success by up to 25%. Conversely, a Low security zone is a soft target – spy intel that reveals zone security levels is enormously valuable for planning strikes.
trans-unit.target: Zone security is the primary defensive mechanic. A zone at Critical security reduces enemy operative success by up to 25%. Conversely, a Low security zone is a soft target – spy intel that reveals zone security levels is enormously valuable for planning strikes.

trans-unit.source: Agent memory uses a dual-layer architecture: observations are raw factual records ("I deployed as a spy against The Gaslit Reach"), while reflections are synthesized interpretations ("My infiltration revealed a pattern of weak northern defenses – the enemy is concentrating forces in the south"). Reflections are AI-generated from accumulated observations.
trans-unit.target: Agent memory uses a dual-layer architecture: observations are raw factual records ("I deployed as a spy against The Gaslit Reach"), while reflections are synthesized interpretations ("My infiltration revealed a pattern of weak northern defenses – the enemy is concentrating forces in the south"). Reflections are AI-generated from accumulated observations.

trans-unit.source: The Cartographer’s Map is an interactive force-directed graph that visualizes the entire multiverse – every simulation, their connections, and active game instances. Nodes represent simulations, edges represent cross-dimensional connections, and energy pulses flow along the connections to show bleed activity.
trans-unit.target: The Cartographer’s Map is an interactive force-directed graph that visualizes the entire multiverse – every simulation, their connections, and active game instances. Nodes represent simulations, edges represent cross-dimensional connections, and energy pulses flow along the connections to show bleed activity.

trans-unit.source: The battle feed ticker at the bottom of the map shows a scrolling log of public battle events across all active epochs – useful for spectators following multiple matches.
trans-unit.target: The battle feed ticker at the bottom of the map shows a scrolling log of public battle events across all active epochs – useful for spectators following multiple matches.

trans-unit.source: Bot players are AI opponents that can be added to any epoch. Each bot has a personality archetype that determines its strategic behavior – from defensive fortress-builders to chaotic wild cards. Bots use the exact same deployment and scoring mechanics as human players, ensuring fair competition.
trans-unit.target: Bot players are AI opponents that can be added to any epoch. Each bot has a personality archetype that determines its strategic behavior – from defensive fortress-builders to chaotic wild cards. Bots use the exact same deployment and scoring mechanics as human players, ensuring fair competition.

trans-unit.source: Bots can participate in epoch chat with two modes. Template mode generates instant, cost-free responses based on personality templates. LLM mode uses OpenRouter to produce context-aware, strategically nuanced messages – bots will taunt, negotiate, and bluff based on the game state. The mode is configurable per simulation in AI Settings.
trans-unit.target: Bots can participate in epoch chat with two modes. Template mode generates instant, cost-free responses based on personality templates. LLM mode uses OpenRouter to produce context-aware, strategically nuanced messages – bots will taunt, negotiate, and bluff based on the game state. The mode is configurable per simulation in AI Settings.

trans-unit.source: When an agent responds, the system retrieves their most relevant memories using a Stanford Generative Agents-style retrieval formula that balances semantic similarity, importance, and recency. These memories are injected into the agent’s system prompt, so their responses reflect what they’ve experienced – events they witnessed, missions they participated in, and reflections they’ve formed.
trans-unit.target: When an agent responds, the system retrieves their most relevant memories using a Stanford Generative Agents-style retrieval formula that balances semantic similarity, importance, and recency. These memories are injected into the agent’s system prompt, so their responses reflect what they’ve experienced – events they witnessed, missions they participated in, and reflections they’ve formed.

trans-unit.source: Navigate to the Chat tab in your simulation and select an agent from the agent selector. Each conversation is a separate thread – you can maintain multiple ongoing conversations with different agents. Messages appear with directional animations, and AI responses are generated in real time via OpenRouter.
trans-unit.target: Navigate to the Chat tab in your simulation and select an agent from the agent selector. Each conversation is a separate thread – you can maintain multiple ongoing conversations with different agents. Messages appear with directional animations, and AI responses are generated in real time via OpenRouter.

trans-unit.source: Events are the narrative heartbeat of every simulation. They represent things that happen in the world – political upheavals, economic crises, military conflicts, cultural shifts, natural disasters. Events can be created manually, generated by AI, or produced automatically through game mechanics like operative missions and bleed echoes.
trans-unit.target: Events are the narrative heartbeat of every simulation. They represent things that happen in the world – political upheavals, economic crises, military conflicts, cultural shifts, natural disasters. Events can be created manually, generated by AI, or produced automatically through game mechanics like operative missions and bleed echoes.

trans-unit.source: Generate complete events using AI through the event creation modal. The AI produces a title, description, impact level, and tags – all contextually appropriate to your simulation’s theme and current state. Each simulation can configure its own AI model and prompt templates to control the tone and style of generated events.
trans-unit.target: Generate complete events using AI through the event creation modal. The AI produces a title, description, impact level, and tags – all contextually appropriate to your simulation’s theme and current state. Each simulation can configure its own AI model and prompt templates to control the tone and style of generated events.

trans-unit.source: After an event occurs, agents can react to it based on their personality, profession, and background. Agent reactions are AI-generated character-driven responses – a military general reacts differently to an invasion than a merchant or a priest. Reactions appear as expandable cards below the event details.
trans-unit.target: After an event occurs, agents can react to it based on their personality, profession, and background. Agent reactions are AI-generated character-driven responses – a military general reacts differently to an invasion than a merchant or a priest. Reactions appear as expandable cards below the event details.

trans-unit.source: Events with high impact levels (8+) can trigger bleed echoes to connected simulations, cascade events in stressed zones, and shift epoch scoring dimensions. They’re not just narrative – they have real mechanical consequences.
trans-unit.target: Events with high impact levels (8+) can trigger bleed echoes to connected simulations, cascade events in stressed zones, and shift epoch scoring dimensions. They’re not just narrative – they have real mechanical consequences.

trans-unit.source: Every simulation has a dedicated Lore section – an in-world narrative of 3,000–5,000 words organized into six chapters. Lore describes the world’s history, geography, political systems, culture, and defining tensions. It’s the first thing visitors see when entering a simulation, setting the tone for everything that follows.
trans-unit.target: Every simulation has a dedicated Lore section – an in-world narrative of 3,000–5,000 words organized into six chapters. Lore describes the world’s history, geography, political systems, culture, and defining tensions. It’s the first thing visitors see when entering a simulation, setting the tone for everything that follows.
